description_long: This house features a sprawling, multi-sectioned layout with a complex roofline composed of intersecting gable and hip elements, all covered in dark gray shingles. The exterior walls are finished in a reddish-brown brick, giving the structure a traditional and sturdy appearance. Large garage doors dominate one side, while the opposite side reveals a mix of windows and doors, including a prominent bay window and a secondary entrance. Blue trim accents the roof edges and railings, adding a touch of color contrast to the otherwise earthy palette. The house has multiple visible entry points, with a main entryway set beneath a gabled section and several secondary doors along the sides. The window arrangement is varied, with both symmetrical and asymmetrical placements, and the overall design suggests a blend of Colonial and Ranch architectural influences.
description: A large, brick house with a complex gabled and hipped roof, blue trim, multiple garages, and varied window arrangements. The structure features both main and secondary entryways, with a mix of traditional and modern design elements.
description_view_0: This view highlights the three large garage doors and the intricate, multi-peaked roofline with blue trim.
description_view_1: This side shows a mix of windows and doors, including a prominent bay window and a secondary entrance, as well as the blue railing along the roof edge.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals additional garage doors, a small covered entry, and the complex roof geometry with intersecting gables.
description_view_3: This angle emphasizes the extended length of the house, the bay window, and the secondary entrance, with fewer visible windows.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with bay window and mixed sizes
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry beneath a gabled section, secondary doors, no porch, blue trim, and minimal decorative elements
